name: Rota's Basis Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: the set of all bases of a matroid M, closed under the exchange axiom

VINIT.justification: void/initial state before any elements are selected; the uninitialized ground set

TANCH.chosen_element: the matroid M itself (the complete axiomatic closure)
TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains and validates all basis operations; the hermetic vessel of the exchange property

AFWD.chosen_element: augmentation: adding an element e ∉ B to basis B to form a larger independent set
AFWD.justification: forward morphism toward completion; directed growth of independent sets

AREV.chosen_element: deletion: removing an element f ∈ B from basis B to form a smaller independent set
AREV.justification: reverse/descent transformation; parity flip from augmentation

CLINK.chosen_element: the exchange operation itself: (B − f) ∪ e where f ∈ B, e ∉ B, and the result remains a basis
CLINK.justification: composition of augmentation and deletion; sequential chaining of morphisms into a single atomic exchange

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: basis B recognizing itself as a maximal independent set of rank r(M)
IMSCRIB.justification: identity/self-reference; the basis is itself, invariant under the exchange property

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the exchange axiom: given bases B₁ and B₂, for any e ∈ B₁ − B₂, there exists f ∈ B₂ − B₁ such that (B₁ − e) ∪ f is a basis
FSPLIT.justification: splits the problem into two branches: (1) the existence of e in the symmetric difference, (2) the existence of f that preserves basis property

FFUSE.chosen_element: the reconstituted basis (B₁ − e) ∪ f, which is guaranteed to be a basis by the exchange axiom
FFUSE.justification: fuses the two branches back into a single basis; FFUSE(FSPLIT(B₁, B₂)) = a valid basis, recovering the closure property

EVALT.chosen_element: successful exchange: f ∈ B₂ − B₁ exists and (B₁ − e) ∪ f is indeed a basis
EVALT.justification: affirmative/success state; the exchange property holds, matroid axioms are satisfied

EVALF.chosen_element: failed exchange: no such f exists, or (B₁ − e) ∪ f violates independence
EVALF.justification: negative/failure state; the structure is not a matroid, exchange axiom is violated

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the paradice of exchange: both B₁ and B₂ are simultaneously valid bases, yet the exchange creates a third basis (B₁ − e) ∪ f that is neither purely B₁ nor B₂
ENGAGR.justification: both states held without resolution; the matroid lattice contains multiple incomparable bases, all equally valid

IFIX.chosen_element: the permanent record of a basis B as a maximal independent set; once certified, it cannot be uncertified
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation; append-only record of basis membership, irreversible commitment to the rank and structure

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: the exchange axiom branches the problem into (1) finding e ∈ B₁ − B₂ and (2) finding f ∈ B₂ − B₁
phase_2.split_input: two bases B₁ and B₂ of the same matroid M
split_outputs: T-branch: e ∈ B₁ − B₂ is identified; augmentation path is open
F-branch: f ∈ B₂ − B₁ is identified; deletion path is open
phase_2.fuse_element: FFUSE: the exchange operation (B₁ − e) ∪ f reconstitutes a basis
phase_2.fuse_result: a new basis B₃ = (B₁ − e) ∪ f that is guaranteed to be a basis by the exchange axiom; the two branches fuse back into a single valid basis, semantically equal to the input pair (B₁, B₂) in terms of rank and matroid closure

phase_3.void_description: 00 — no basis selected, no independent set formed; the empty ground set before any matroid structure is imposed
phase_3.true_description: 01 — a valid basis B exists; the exchange property holds; all axioms are satisfied; rank r(M) is well-defined
phase_3.false_description: 10 — no valid basis exists; the exchange axiom fails; the structure is not a matroid; rank is undefined
phase_3.both_description: 11 — a paradice: multiple incomparable bases coexist in the matroid lattice; the exchange property creates a superposition of valid bases, all simultaneously held without collapse to a single canonical form

steps.domain_action: initialize with the empty independent set ∅; no elements selected, no rank assigned
invoke the matroid M as the boundary; all operations must respect the exchange axiom
given two bases B₁ and B₂, split into two branches: (T) find e ∈ B₁ − B₂, (F) find f ∈ B₂ − B₁
T-branch affirms: e exists in the symmetric difference B₁ − B₂; augmentation is possible
T-branch morphism: move forward by adding e to (B₁ − e) to form an intermediate set
F-branch affirms: f exists in the symmetric difference B₂ − B₁; deletion is possible
F-branch morphism: move backward by removing e from B₁ to form (B₁ − e); parity flip from augmentation
compose the two morphisms into a single exchange operation: (B₁ − e) ∪ f
fuse the two branches back into a single basis B₃ = (B₁ − e) ∪ f; the exchange axiom guarantees this is a basis
B₃ recognizes itself as a maximal independent set of rank r(M); self-reference to the basis property
record B₃ as a permanent, append-only entry in the basis lattice; this basis is now fixed and cannot be uncertified
acknowledge that B₁, B₂, and B₃ are all simultaneously valid bases; the matroid lattice holds multiple incomparable bases without collapse
compose the exchange operation with the identity to form a loop: (B₁, B₂) → B₃ → (B₂, B₃) → B₄, etc.
each new basis recognizes itself as part of the same matroid; the exchange property is self-referential
record each new basis as a permanent entry; the basis lattice grows append-only
forward morphism: move from one basis to another via exchange; the matroid structure is traversable
reverse morphism: move backward from one basis to another via inverse exchange; the matroid lattice is bidirectional
given a basis B and an element e ∉ B, split into two branches: (T) adding e creates a unique circuit C(e), (F) the circuit is empty (contradiction)
T-branch affirms: the circuit C(e) exists; the matroid has a well-defined circuit structure
F-branch affirms: no circuit exists; the element e is independent of B (contradiction to the split premise)
both branches are held simultaneously; the circuit and its absence coexist in the matroid lattice until a specific basis is chosen
fuse the circuit branches back into the basis B; the exchange property ensures that removing any element from C(e) and adding e yields a basis
compose the circuit operation with the basis operation; the matroid is defined by its bases and circuits simultaneously
the matroid recognizes itself as a self-dual structure; bases and circuits are dual concepts
record the circuit structure as a permanent, append-only entry; the matroid's circuit lattice is now fixed
forward morphism: move from the basis lattice to the circuit lattice; the matroid structure is fully traversable
reverse morphism: move from the circuit lattice back to the basis lattice; the duality is bidirectional
given a matroid M, split into two branches: (T) M is realizable over a field F, (F) M is non-realizable (abstract)
T-branch affirms: M has a matrix representation; the exchange property corresponds to linear independence
F-branch affirms: M is abstract; the exchange property is purely combinatorial, not derived from linear algebra
both branches are held simultaneously; the matroid is both realizable and abstract, depending on the representation chosen
fuse the two branches back into the matroid M; the exchange axiom holds regardless of representation
compose the realizability operation with the exchange operation; the matroid is defined by its exchange property, not its representation
the matroid recognizes itself as representation-independent; the exchange property is the canonical definition
record the matroid's exchange axiom as a permanent, append-only entry; the matroid is now fully defined and cannot be redefined
close the system; all bases, circuits, and exchange operations are contained within the matroid M; the boundary is hermetic

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the exchange axiom itself; it translates the intention to move from one basis to another into a concrete operation (B − e) ∪ f
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the symmetric difference B₁ △ B₂; bases communicate by identifying which elements differ and which can be exchanged
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the basis lattice; each basis is stored as a maximal independent set, indexed by rank and element membership
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the matroid rank function r(M); operations are ordered by rank, with lower-rank bases processed before higher-rank bases
phase_5.alfs_store: the set of all bases of M; the canonical reference store that validates all exchange operations

phase_6.cycle_cost: one exchange operation per cycle; the cost is the cardinality of the symmetric difference |B₁ △ B₂|
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: two bases B₁ and B₂ of the same matroid M, with |B₁| = |B₂| = r(M)
phase_6.post_cycle_state: a new basis B₃ = (B₁ − e) ∪ f, with |B₃| = r(M); the rank is preserved
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the exchange operation is entropy-conservative because the rank is invariant; the matroid structure is closed under exchange, and no information is lost or created; the cycle is reversible via the inverse exchange (B₃ − f) ∪ e = B₁

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → AREV → CLINK → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → IFIX → ENGAGR → CLINK → IMSCRIB → IFIX → AFWD → AREV → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → ENGAGR → FFUSE → CLINK → IMSCRIB → IFIX → AFWD → AREV → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → ENGAGR → FFUSE → CLINK → IMSCRIB → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Rota's Basis Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(17,6,9,4)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=3
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=36
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(2, 8), (17, 21), (27, 31)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑚  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [6] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [7] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑭  | identity — self-imscription
--   [10] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑠 → 𐑳  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [11] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑭 → 𐑱  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [12] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑳 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [13] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑭  | identity — self-imscription

--   [17] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition

--   [19] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [20] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms

--   [22] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [23] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑭  | identity — self-imscription
--   [24] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑠 → 𐑾  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [25] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑭 → 𐑗  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [26] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑾 → 𐑚  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [27] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [28] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [29] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [30] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [31] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [32] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [33] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑭  | identity — self-imscription

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair

  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑭 𐑗)  -- [15] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (forward morphism: move from one basis to another via exchange; the matroid st...)

  -- FSPLIT [17] (gran := 𐑚) (given a basis B and an element e ∉ B, split into two branches: (T) adding e creates a unique circuit C(e), (F) the circuit is empty (contradiction)) / FFUSE [21] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [18] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (T-branch affirms: the circuit C(e) exists; the matroid has a well-defined cir...)

    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [31] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (fuse the two branches back into the matroid M; the exchange axiom holds regardless of representation)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [32] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose the realizability operation with the exchange operation; the matroid ...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [33] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the matroid recognizes itself as representation-independent; the exchange pro...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [34] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record the matroid's exchange axiom as a permanent, append-only entry; the ma...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [35] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (close the system; all bases, circuits, and exchange operations are contained ...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def rotas_basis_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (rotas_basis_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def rotas_basis_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (rotas_basis_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem rotas_basis_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

end Imscribing
